Associate Professor in Applied Mathematics, University of Nottingham

Research Portfolio

Our research provides mathematical and computational solutions for resolving complex physical phenomena across scales.

Full Publication Lists

For a complete and up-to-date list of my 85+ publications, please see:

  • Google Scholar Profile
  • Scopus Author ID
  • ResearchGate

Core Research Pillars

  1. Multiscale Modeling & Coarse-Graining: Deriving macroscopic equations from microscopic physics, including Basset-type fractional transport models.
  2. Porous Media & Subsurface Dynamics: Carbon Capture and Storage (CCS), fractured reservoirs, and contaminant hydrology.
  3. Uncertainty Quantification (UQ): Deploying Multilevel Monte Carlo (MLMC) for robust predictions in the presence of sparse data.
  4. Scientific Machine Learning (SciML): Integrating physics into neural networks (PINNs) for inverse transport and modeling problems.

Selected Landmark Publications

  • “From microstructure to memory: Basset-type fractional transport models in porous media”, Proceedings of the Royal Society A (2025).
  • “Inverse Physics-Informed Neural Networks for transport models in porous materials”, Computer Methods in Applied Mechanics and Engineering (2025).
  • “Mechanisms of Dispersion in a Porous Medium”, Journal of Fluid Mechanics (2018).
  • “Pore-scale simulation of fluid flow and solute dispersion in three-dimensional porous media”, Physical Review E (2014).
